content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 15, 2019

Thomas Watson
City Attorney
333 Civic Center Plaza
Tracy, CA 95376

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-19-026

Dear Mr. Watson:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Since your advice does not refer to a specific governmental decision but seeks general guidance, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.²

Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090.

Also note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for additional advice.

QUESTION

May Tracy City Councilmember Veronica Vargas take part in governmental decisions as an alternate on the South San Joaquin County Fire Authority Board, given that her husband is a Division Chief for the City's Fire Department?

CONCLUSION

Because her husband's government salary is not a potentially disqualifying source of income economic interest under the Act, Councilmember Vargas may take part in governmental

_____________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

File No. 1-19-026
Page No. 2

decisions as an alternate board member to the extent that the decisions will not have reasonably
foreseeable, material effects on her personal finances.

FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER

Veronica Vargas is a City Councilmember in the City of Tracy. Councilmember Vargas's
spouse (Pat Vargas) is one of two Division Chiefs in the City fire department. He is a management
employee and not part of a bargaining unit. The City is the employer of record for all fire
employees who are assigned to the South San Joaquin County Fire Authority (SSJCFA), including
Chief Vargas.

The SSJCFA is governed by a board consisting of four members, including two City
Councilmembers and two members from the Tracy Rural Fire District. In January 2019,
Councilmember Vargas was selected by the City Council as an alternate to the SSJCFA Board, to
serve if either of the two City Council members who were designated to be the City's
representatives to the SSJCFA are unable to attend a meeting.

The SSJCFA Board provides oversight to the appointed Chief Executive Officer/Fire Chief
("CEO/FC") for the SSJCFA, as well as setting budget and strategic priorities. The Board only has
employment authority over the CEO/FC (a position distinct from Division Chief), but does control
the budget for staffing and operations.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the
official has a financial interest. Section 87103 provides that an official has a "financial interest" in a
decision, within the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a
material financial effect on one or more of the official's interests. Interests from which a conflict of
interest may arise are defined in Section 87103 and, as pertinent to the facts provided, include the
following:

• A public official's own finances, or those of a member of his or her immediate family.

• Any source of income aggregating five hundred dollars ($500) or more in value provided or
promised to, received by, the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

In general, "income," within the meaning of the Act, includes any community property
interest in the income of a spouse. (Section 82030(a).) However, Section 82030(b)(2) provides that
"income" does not include "[s]alary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem received from a
state, local, or federal government agency...." Therefore, Councilmember Vargas does not have a
potentially disqualifying source of income interest in her husband's salary.

As an alternate on the SSJCFA Board, Councilmember Vargas will be disqualified from a
governmental decision only to the extent that it will have a reasonably foreseeable, material effect
on her personal finances. However, a personal financial effect does not include "[a]ny establishment
of or change to benefits provided under an employment or retirement policy for employees or

File No. 1-19-026
Page No. 3

retirees if the financial effect of the decision applies equally to all employees in the same bargaining unit or other representative group." (Regulation 18702.5(b)(1).) Additionally, we note that while a decision affecting governmental salary generally will not be disqualifying, an official may be disqualified from a decision based upon an effect on the official's personal finances if the decision is one to appoint, hire, fire, promote, demote, suspend without pay, or take a disciplinary action with financial sanction against the official or his or her immediate family, or set salary for the official or his or her immediate family different from salaries paid to other employees of the agency in the same job classification or position. (See Regulation 18704(d)(3).)

Given the above, Councilmember Vargas may take part in a governmental decision as an alternate on the SSJCFA Board to the extent that the decision would not have a reasonably foreseeable, material effect on her personal finances. If Councilmember Vargas is presented with a specific governmental decision and is unsure whether it would have a reasonably foreseeable, material impact on her personal finances, she may wish to seek additional advice prior to taking part in the decision.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Dave Bainbridge
General Counsel

By: Kevin Cornwall
Counsel, Legal Division

KMC:jgl
